Request For Entry Of Court Default Judgment At Prove Up Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-17-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Real Bright Media, Inc., a California corporation, and Joshua Bass.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Linda Forster, CSR #13286. Email: lforster@sftc.org.

Daniel L. Balsam, Esq. and Jacob Harker, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Fenwick Crecy, who was placed under oath and tesified.
Messrs. Balsam, Harker and Crecy all appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendants Real Bright Media, Inc., a California corporation, or Joshua Bass.

Plaintiff's Second Amended for Violations of California Restrictions on Unsolicited Commercial E-Mail (Cal.Bus. & Prof. Code Section 17529.5) was filed on March 28, 2016, and personally served on defendants Real Bright Media, Inc., a California corporation, and Joshua Bass on March 28, 2016 and April 7, 2016, respectively.
Default as to defendant Joshua Bass was entered on June 29, 2016.
Default as to defendant Real Bright Media, Inc., a California corporation, was entered on August 9, 2016.
DOE defendants were dismissed on May 11, 2021.
Dismissal Without Prejudice as to plaintiffs Matt Barrett, Heather Byrnes and Nicole Morton were filed with the Clerk's Office on February 6, 2020.
Dismissal Without Prejudice as to plaintiff Cynthia Hechek was filed with the Clerk's Office on June 26, 2020.

The Court GRANTS plaintiff Request for Judicial Notice, and signs the Order Granting Request for Judicial Notice Supporting Request for entry of court Default Judgment at Prove-Up Hearing.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Fenwick Crecy, and against the defaulted defendants Real Bright Media, Inc., a California corporation, and Joshua Bass, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $76,000.00.
Costs - $1,531.39.

Total - $77,531.39.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.
